node.title: Uber Codesignal – Solved 3/4 and 4/4, but No Response Yet

article.title: Uber Codesignal – Solved 3/4 and 4/4, but No Response Yet
article.content:
Hey everyone,

I wanted to check if anyone else has faced something similar with Uber’s Codesignal.

I have given the Uber Codesignal twice in the last 6 months for sde2 Backend roles.

The first time, I managed to solve 3 out of 4 questions.

The second time, I was able to solve all 4 questions within the time.

Despite that, I haven’t received any invite for the next rounds or further process.

I’m trying to figure out whether this is common. Does it depend on other factors like resume screening, location, or headcount availability?

If you’ve taken the Uber Codesignal recently, it would be really helpful to know:

How many questions you solved and whether you got a call back.


Whether Uber tends to reject candidates even with good Codesignal scores because of timing or internal hiring needs.

Just wanted to see if this has happened with others too. Any insights would be appreciated!

post.content: Same thing happened with me, you\'re not alone buddy!
I have solved codesignal 1,2,4 and scored 510 ish. They moved me to phone screen and then on-site promptly. Did you reach out to your recruiter? How did you apply for role, need more details
Uber always do this. The code signal assessment is only a entry point. At least for new grads positions, they only proceed limit amount of people even the OA is full mark.

although this is sad , some reasons can be.. 
- role got filled INTERNALLY ( someone from inside the company got transferred to this team )
- they going ahead with some other candidates who also solved 4/4 questions, 
- role got de-prioritized ( ie due to budget , or shifted to next quarter or whatever )

it is extremely rare that you are the only one applying for that role, recruiters keep hunting for people all day long and these big companies have 100s if not 1000s of people in the pipeline at any given time.
